1→# Ploy API - High Availability Nomad Job
     2→# 
     3→# This job deploys the Ploy API with:
     4→# - High availability across multiple nodes
     5→# - Rolling updates with health checks
     6→# - Binary distribution via SeaweedFS
     7→# - Service discovery integration
     8→# - Load balancing via Traefik
     9→
    10→job "ploy-api" {
    11→  datacenters = ["dc1"]
    12→  type = "service"
    13→  priority = 80
    14→
    15→  # High availability configuration
    16→  # Run multiple instances across different nodes (production) or single instance (dev)
    17→  group "api" {
    18→    {% if ploy_environment is defined and ploy_environment == 'dev' %}
    19→    count = {{ nomad_api.replicas | default(1) }}
    20→    {% else %}
    21→    count = {{ nomad_api.replicas | default(2) }}
    22→
    23→    # Spread across different nodes for availability (production only)
    24→    constraint {
    25→      distinct_hosts = true
    26→    }
    27→    {% endif %}
    28→
    29→    # Rolling update configuration
    30→    update {
    31→      max_parallel = 1
    32→      min_healthy_time = "5s"    # Reduced from 10s since API starts quickly
    33→      healthy_deadline = "10m"    # Increased from 5m for initial health checks
    34→      progress_deadline = "15m"   # Increased from 10m to allow more time
    35→      auto_revert = true
    36→      canary = 1
    37→    }
    38→
    39→    # Network configuration (moved from task level to group level)
    40→    network {
    41→      port "http" {}  # Dynamic port allocation
    42→    }
    43→
    44→    # Restart policy
    45→    restart {
    46→      attempts = 3
    47→      interval = "10m"
    48→      delay = "30s"
    49→      mode = "fail"
    50→    }

content.thinking: Now let me create the Nomad job template for the SeaweedFS filer based on the API template pattern. I need to create a new template file that will run the SeaweedFS filer as a Nomad service.

content.text: Now I'll create the Nomad job template for the SeaweedFS filer:

input.content:
# SeaweedFS Filer - Nomad Service Job
# 
# This job deploys the SeaweedFS Filer with:
# - Service discovery via Consul DNS (seaweedfs-filer.service.consul)
# - Integration with systemd-managed master and volume servers
# - Health checks and automatic recovery
# - Binary distribution via SeaweedFS artifact download

job "seaweedfs-filer" {
  datacenters = ["dc1"]
  type = "service"
  priority = 75

  # Single instance for development, can be scaled in production
  group "filer" {
    count = 1

    # Rolling update configuration
    update {
      max_parallel = 1
      min_healthy_time = "10s"
      healthy_deadline = "5m"
      progress_deadline = "10m"
      auto_revert = true
    }

    # Network configuration - use static port for SeaweedFS filer
    network {
      port "filer" {
        static = {{ seaweedfs.filer_port }}
      }
    }

    # Restart policy for stability
    restart {
      attempts = 5
      interval = "10m"
      delay = "30s"
      mode = "fail"
    }

    task "filer" {
      driver = "raw_exec"

      # Download SeaweedFS binary
      artifact {
        source = "https://github.com/seaweedfs/seaweedfs/releases/download/{{ seaweedfs.version }}/linux_amd64.tar.gz"
        destination = "local/"
        mode = "any"
      }

      # Environment variables for filer configuration
      env {
        SEAWEEDFS_MASTER = "localhost:{{ seaweedfs.master_port }}"
        SEAWEEDFS_DATA_CENTER = "dc1"
        SEAWEEDFS_RACK = "rack1"
        SEAWEEDFS_FILER_PORT = "{{ seaweedfs.filer_port }}"
      }

      # Configuration for raw_exec driver
      config {
        command = "/bin/bash"
        args = ["local/start-filer.sh"]
      }

      # Resource allocation
      resources {
        cpu = 300    # MHz - lighter than API
        memory = 256 # MB - filer is lightweight
      }

      # Create startup script
      template {
        data = <<EOF
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Ensure master is accessible before starting filer
echo "[FILER] Waiting for SeaweedFS master at localhost:{{ seaweedfs.master_port }}..."
timeout 60 bash -c 'until nc -z localhost {{ seaweedfs.master_port }}; do sleep 2; done'
echo "[FILER] ✓ Master is accessible"

# Create filer data directory
mkdir -p /var/lib/seaweedfs/filer
chown seaweedfs:seaweedfs /var/lib/seaweedfs/filer || true

# Extract and prepare SeaweedFS binary
echo "[FILER] Extracting SeaweedFS binary..."
tar -xzf linux_amd64.tar.gz
chmod +x weed

# Start SeaweedFS filer
echo "[FILER] Starting SeaweedFS filer on port {{ seaweedfs.filer_port }}..."
exec ./weed filer \
  -port={{ seaweedfs.filer_port }} \
  -ip.bind=0.0.0.0 \
  -master=localhost:{{ seaweedfs.master_port }} \
  -dataCenter=dc1 \
  -defaultReplicaPlacement=000 \
  -dirListLimit=1000 \
  -defaultStoreDir=/var/lib/seaweedfs/filer
EOF
        destination = "local/start-filer.sh"
        perms = "755"
      }

      # Health checks and service discovery
      service {
        name = "seaweedfs-filer"
        port = "filer"
        tags = [
          "seaweedfs",
          "filer", 
          "storage"
        ]

        # HTTP health check
        check {
          type = "http"
          path = "/"
          interval = "10s"
          timeout = "5s"
          port = "filer"
        }

        # TCP health check as backup
        check {
          name = "filer-tcp"
          type = "tcp"
          interval = "15s"
          timeout = "5s"
          port = "filer"
        }
        
        # Configure Consul to automatically deregister failed services
        meta {
          deregister_critical_service_after = "30s"
        }
      }

      # Graceful shutdown
      kill_timeout = "30s"
      kill_signal = "SIGTERM"

      # Logging configuration
      logs {
        max_files = 5
        max_file_size = 10
      }
    }
  }
}

content.content:
# - Builds API binary and distribution tools
# - Uploads API binary to SeaweedFS storage
# - Deploys API as Nomad job with high availability
# - Validates API deployment and health
# - Implements proper service ordering and dependencies
#
- name: Deploy Ploy API via Nomad
  hosts: linux_hosts
--
              api_version: {{ binary_version.stdout }}
        
        - name: Create Nomad job for API deployment
          template:
            src: ../../common/templates/nomad-ploy-api.hcl.j2
            dest: /opt/hashicorp/nomad/jobs/ploy-api.hcl
            owner: ploy
            group: ploy
--
        - name: Copy job manager script
          copy:
            src: /Users/vk/@iw2rmb/ploy/iac/common/scripts/nomad-job-manager.sh
            dest: /opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh
            mode: '0755'
            owner: root
            group: root
        - name: Copy service monitoring script
--
        - name: Stop existing API job if running
          shell: /opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh stop --job ploy-api
          environment:
            NOMAD_ADDR: "http://localhost:4646"
          register: stop_result
          failed_when: false
--
        - name: Deploy API job using HTTP API
          shell: /opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh run --job ploy-api --file /opt/hashicorp/nomad/jobs/ploy-api.hcl
          environment:
            NOMAD_ADDR: "http://localhost:4646"
          register: bootstrap_deploy
        - name: Wait for bootstrap allocation to be running
          shell: /opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh wait --job ploy-api --timeout 300
          environment:
            NOMAD_ADDR: "http://localhost:4646"
          register: wait_result
          failed_when: wait_result.rc != 0
        - name: Get allocation status for verification
          shell: /opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh status --job ploy-api
          environment:
            NOMAD_ADDR: "http://localhost:4646"
          register: bootstrap_allocs
          failed_when: false
--
          Bootstrap API deployment completed successfully:
          - Version: {{ current_deployment_version }}
          - Deployment method: Bootstrap Nomad job (unified deployment system)
          - Health endpoint: https://api.dev.{{ ploy.platform_domain | default('ployman.app', true) }}/health
          - External access: https://api.dev.ployman.app/health
          - Management tools: Available in /home/ploy/api-scripts/
          
          NEXT STEPS:

Perfect! Now I have enough information to create the seaweedfs-filer.yml playbook. I can see the pattern:

1. Create the Nomad job file from the template
2. Use the nomad-job-manager.sh script to deploy
3. Wait for the job to be running
4. Verify deployment

Let me create the seaweedfs-filer.yml playbook based on this pattern.

content.text: Now I'll create the seaweedfs-filer.yml playbook based on the API deployment pattern:

input.content:
---
# SeaweedFS Filer Deployment via Nomad
#
# This playbook deploys the SeaweedFS Filer as a Nomad service:
# - Ensures master and volume servers are running via systemd
# - Deploys filer as Nomad job for better integration and management
# - Configures Consul service discovery for seaweedfs-filer.service.consul
# - Validates filer deployment and health checks
#
- name: Deploy SeaweedFS Filer via Nomad
  hosts: linux_hosts
  become: true
  vars_files:
    - ../vars/main.yml
  
  tasks:
    - name: Ensure SeaweedFS master and volume servers are running
      block:
        - name: Wait for SeaweedFS master to be healthy
          wait_for:
            port: "{{ seaweedfs.master_port }}"
            delay: 2
            timeout: 60
          
        - name: Wait for SeaweedFS volume server to be healthy
          wait_for:
            port: "{{ seaweedfs.volume_port }}"
            delay: 2
            timeout: 60
          
        - name: Verify master cluster status
          uri:
            url: "http://localhost:{{ seaweedfs.master_port }}/cluster/status"
            method: GET
          register: cluster_status
          failed_when: cluster_status.status != 200

        - name: Display SeaweedFS cluster readiness
          debug:
            msg: "✅ SeaweedFS master and volume servers are ready for filer deployment"

    - name: Ensure Nomad is ready for filer deployment
      block:
        - name: Verify Nomad cluster is ready
          uri:
            url: "http://localhost:4646/v1/status/leader"
            method: GET
            status_code: 200
          retries: 5
          delay: 10

        - name: Display Nomad readiness status
          debug:
            msg: "✅ Nomad cluster is ready for filer deployment"

    - name: Deploy SeaweedFS filer via Nomad
      block:
        - name: Create Nomad job for SeaweedFS filer
          template:
            src: ../../common/templates/nomad-seaweedfs-filer.hcl.j2
            dest: /opt/hashicorp/nomad/jobs/seaweedfs-filer.hcl
            owner: ploy
            group: ploy
            mode: '0644'

        - name: Validate job syntax
          shell: nomad job validate /opt/hashicorp/nomad/jobs/seaweedfs-filer.hcl
          environment:
            NOMAD_ADDR: "http://localhost:4646"
          become_user: ploy

        - name: Copy job manager script (if not exists)
          copy:
            src: ../../common/scripts/nomad-job-manager.sh
            dest: /opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh
            mode: '0755'
            owner: root
            group: root
            force: false

        - name: Stop existing filer job if running
          shell: /opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh stop --job seaweedfs-filer
          environment:
            NOMAD_ADDR: "http://localhost:4646"
          register: stop_result
          failed_when: false

        - name: Wait between stop and deploy operations
          pause:
            seconds: 5

        - name: Deploy SeaweedFS filer job
          shell: /opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh run --job seaweedfs-filer --file /opt/hashicorp/nomad/jobs/seaweedfs-filer.hcl
          environment:
            NOMAD_ADDR: "http://localhost:4646"
          register: filer_deploy

        - name: Wait for filer allocation to be running
          shell: /opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh wait --job seaweedfs-filer --timeout 120
          environment:
            NOMAD_ADDR: "http://localhost:4646"
          register: wait_result
          failed_when: wait_result.rc != 0

        - name: Get allocation status for verification
          shell: /opt/hashicorp/bin/nomad-job-manager.sh status --job seaweedfs-filer
          environment:
            NOMAD_ADDR: "http://localhost:4646"
          register: filer_allocs
          failed_when: false

        - name: Display filer deployment status
          debug:
            msg: "SeaweedFS filer deployed successfully via Nomad"

    - name: Validate SeaweedFS filer service
      block:
        - name: Wait for filer to be available via Consul DNS
          wait_for:
            host: "seaweedfs-filer.service.consul"
            port: "{{ seaweedfs.filer_port }}"
            delay: 10
            timeout: 60
          register: consul_dns_check
          ignore_errors: true

        - name: Test filer HTTP endpoint directly
          uri:
            url: "http://localhost:{{ seaweedfs.filer_port }}/"
            method: GET
            status_code: 200
          register: filer_http_check
          retries: 5
          delay: 10

        - name: Verify filer is registered in Consul
          uri:
            url: "http://localhost:8500/v1/catalog/service/seaweedfs-filer"
            method: GET
            status_code: 200
          register: consul_service_check

        - name: Display final validation results
          debug:
            msg: |
              ✅ SeaweedFS Filer Deployment Complete:
              - Nomad job status: {{ 'running' if wait_result.rc == 0 else 'failed' }}
              - HTTP endpoint: {{ 'healthy' if filer_http_check.status == 200 else 'failed' }}
              - Consul registration: {{ 'registered' if consul_service_check.status == 200 else 'failed' }}
              - Consul DNS: {{ 'accessible' if consul_dns_check is success else 'not accessible (expected during bootstrap)' }}
              - Access via: seaweedfs-filer.service.consul:{{ seaweedfs.filer_port }}

      rescue:
        - name: Display troubleshooting information
          debug:
            msg: |
              ⚠️  Filer deployment completed but validation failed.
              Check the following:
              1. Nomad job status: nomad job status seaweedfs-filer
              2. Service logs: nomad alloc logs <alloc-id>
              3. Consul services: consul catalog services
              4. Port accessibility: curl http://localhost:{{ seaweedfs.filer_port }}/
